---
title: "Merging data"
author: "Jessica Larrick"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r duplicates}
response_actions_filtered_clean <- response_actions_filtered %>%
  group_by(`Public Water System ID`, `Reporting Month`) %>%
  summarise(
    across(starts_with("Used_"), ~ any(. == TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)),  # TRUE if used at least once
    Vigilance = mean(Vigilance, na.rm = TRUE),                     # average of non-NA values(however vigilance measure is not used in paper)
    .groups = "drop"
  )

```

```{r structuring the 2020-2023 data to merge, creating tactic 'retention intensity'}
#plan: for each supplier/year, create a variable that counts how many months they used that tactic in 2020-2023, and then attach that count to every row in the main dataset
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Step 1: Extract year from Reporting Month
response_actions_filtered_clean <- response_actions_filtered_clean %>%
  mutate(Year = format(as.Date(`Reporting Month`), "%Y"))

# Step 2: Count how many months each supplier used each tactic in each year
tactic_counts_by_year <- response_actions_filtered_clean %>%
  filter(Year %in% c("2020", "2021", "2022", "2023")) %>%
  group_by(`Public Water System ID`, Year) %>%
  summarise(
    across(starts_with("Used_"), ~ sum(. == TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)),
    Vigilance_Avg = mean(Vigilance, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Step 3: Pivot wider to get one row per supplier with year-specific columns
tactic_counts_wide <- tactic_counts_by_year %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Year,
    values_from = c(Vigilance_Avg, starts_with("Used_")),
    names_glue = "{.value}_{Year}"
  )

```

```{r merge by supplier ID tactic_counts_wide with my main df "merged_data"}
merged_final <- merged_data %>%
  left_join(tactic_counts_wide, by = c("WATER_SYSTEM_ID" = "Public Water System ID"))

```
